skill.name: designing-tests
skill.description: Designs practical test strategies, selects test frameworks, and provides templates for unit, integration, E2E, coverage, fixtures, and mocks.
skill.summary: Create balanced test plans and implementation patterns for software projects.
skill.author: CloudAI-X

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backticks, weak cryptography keywords, reconnaissance terms, and HTTP patterns. Manual review found Markdown test examples, local test commands, and mock HTTP handlers, with no malicious intent or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command detections point to Markdown fences, JavaScript examples, JSON examples, and inline test commands. The skill does not execute these snippets by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched backticks are visible Markdown code fences and inline examples. No executable wrapper, script file, or hidden automation is present.

low_findings.title: Test Command Guidance Requires Normal Local Execution
low_findings.description: The skill recommends commands such as npm test and coverage runs. This is expected for a testing skill, but users should run commands only in trusted repositories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and limited to test execution. They can run project-defined scripts, so the operational risk is real but normal for test automation.

low_findings.title: HTTP Patterns Are Local Testing Examples
low_findings.description: The network detections are SuperTest style request usage and an MSW mock handler. They demonstrate local API testing and do not contact an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The HTTP examples use relative paths and mocking libraries. There is no external URL, credential handling, or data exfiltration pattern.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain the skill name, describe blocks, test names, and object spread syntax. No cryptographic algorithm usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, DES, SHA1, cipher, hash, or crypto API calls. The matches appear to be substrings inside documentation and sample tests.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The flagged reconnaissance lines are testing pyramid guidance and sample test text. No command gathers host, network, process, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is instructional testing material. No system inspection commands or data collection logic are present.

content.value_statement: Teams often miss important behavior when tests grow without a plan. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design structured test coverage.
actual_capabilities: Maps work to unit, integration, E2E, component, and API test types.
Recommends JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, and Go testing frameworks.
Provides templates for unit, integration, and E2E test structure.
Defines coverage targets for business logic, edge cases, and API contracts.
Guides test data, fixtures, factories, mocks, and validation loops.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a complete test runner.
Framework recommendations may need adjustment for unusual project stacks.
It cannot guarantee coverage quality without reading the target codebase.
Local test commands can execute project scripts in the current repository.

use_cases.title: Add Tests to a Feature
use_cases.description: Plan unit, integration, and E2E coverage before implementing tests for new behavior.

use_cases.title: Improve Coverage Standards
use_cases.description: Set practical thresholds and identify high-value paths that need stronger tests.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Test Suites
use_cases.description: Review fixtures, mocks, data setup, and validation steps to reduce flaky results.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Test Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a test plan for this feature. Include unit, integration, and E2E tests with clear priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Test Frameworks
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project stack and recommend suitable test frameworks. Explain the tradeoffs for each test type.

prompt_templates.title: Design Coverage Targets
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this module and propose coverage targets for business logic, edge cases, API contracts, and user paths.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Test Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this test suite design for gaps, flaky risks, fixture quality, mocking boundaries, and validation workflow.

output_examples.input: Plan tests for a user registration flow.
output_examples.output: A testing pyramid with unit checks for validation, integration checks for account creation, and E2E coverage for successful signup.

output_examples.input: Improve coverage for a payment module.
output_examples.output: A prioritized coverage plan for business rules, error handling, API contracts, retries, and critical checkout paths.

output_examples.input: Reduce flaky tests in a dashboard project.
output_examples.output: A stabilization checklist covering deterministic data, isolated tests, controlled time, clear mocks, and repeated validation runs.

best_practices: Start with business logic and critical user paths before broad snapshot coverage.
Keep mocks at system boundaries and avoid mocking the code under test.
Run coverage and repeated test passes before treating new tests as complete.
anti_patterns: Do not chase coverage percentages while ignoring important behavior.
Do not make tests depend on execution order or shared mutable state.
Do not run project test commands in repositories you do not trust.

faq.question: Does this skill write runnable tests?
faq.answer: It provides test structure and implementation guidance. The assistant still needs the target project files to write exact tests.

faq.question: Which languages does it cover?
faq.answer: The guide includes JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, and Go recommendations.

faq.question: Does it support E2E testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes E2E placement, user journey guidance, and Playwright style examples.

faq.question: Can it help with coverage thresholds?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests practical targets for global coverage and stricter core module coverage.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The HTTP examples are local testing and mock handler examples.

faq.question: What security caution applies?
faq.answer: Review project scripts before running npm test or coverage commands in an unknown repository.
